## Analysis
<!-- ANALYSIS:START — Claude-maintained, preserved across refreshes -->

_Disclaimer: not a coach's analysis. Pattern-reading from the data above. Verify with her actual coach._

### What the data says she's good at

| Event | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **100 SC Breast** | Textbook progression: 1:51.69 → 1:49.41 → 1:46.95 (−4.74s, drops every meet, never regressed) |
| **50 SC Breast** | 46.97 — faster than Kennedy (48.85) despite being slower on nearly every other event |
| **400 SC Free** | −22.01s in 2½ months (7:17.99 → 6:55.98) |
| **Distance commitment** | Only one of the three to race 800 and 1500 Free. Kids who willingly attempt 1500 at age 10 are rare |
| **100 LC Free** | −9.99s in 10 months (1:33.56 → 1:23.57) — matches Kennedy's trajectory |

### Profile vs. cohort

Compare on matching events:

| Event | Anya | Kennedy | Arina |
|---|---|---|---|
| 50 SC Free | 40.83 | **36.40** | 37.89 |
| 50 SC Breast | 55.31 | 48.85 | **46.97** |
| 100 SC Breast | 2:03.07 | **1:45.69** | 1:46.95 |
| 200 SC Free | — | **2:59.79** | 3:01.06 |
| 400 SC Free | — | — | **6:55.98** (only Arina) |
| 200 SC IM | — | **3:19.84** | 3:24.10 |

Kennedy is the generalist. Arina is **breast-stronger and distance-leaning**. Breast-to-free ratio (50 Breast / 50 Free = 46.97/37.89 = **1.24**) is noticeably lower than Kennedy (1.34) or Anya (1.35) — signature ratio of a natural breaststroker.

Projected long-term event mix: 100/200 Breast, 400/800 Free, 200/400 IM. Different trajectory from Kennedy.

### Gaps — significant

**1. Back record is stale and thin.**
- Only LC back swim: 1:58.94 on 100 Back (June 2025, 10 months old)
- Only SC back swim: 47.62 on 50 Back (Feb 13, 2026)
- No 100 SC Back at all; no 200 Back
- 50 SC Back 47.62 is 6+ seconds slower than Kennedy (41.22) where they're only 1.5s apart on Free

Biggest development gap. Can't grow into a 400 IMer without Back.

**2. Butterfly is almost non-existent.**
Zero 50 SC Fly swims. Only Fly in the record: 50 LC Fly 49.17 at Spring Inv (first-ever). No 100 Fly anywhere. More Fly-avoidant than Kennedy. For an IM/breast profile this will cap 200 IM development.

**3. No 100 SC Free.**
Has 200 SC Free and LC 100 Free but somehow no 100 SC Free. Standard event — odd absence; missing the sprint→mid SC benchmark.

**4. LC data mostly stale.**
Six of nine LC swims are from June 2025. Only fresh LC (Apr 2026): 100 Free, 50 Fly, 200 Breast. LC Back/Breast times no longer representative.

### SC vs. LC sanity check

100 SC Breast (Feb 2026) 1:46.95 vs 100 LC Breast (Jun 2025) 2:01.30 — 14.35s gap, but LC time is 10-month-old novice swim. **Ballpark projection:** current 100 LC Breast if raced today would be ~1:52–1:55. Big drop should post at Summer LC Inv.

### Areas of focus — ranked by likely impact

1. **Build Back into her repertoire** — biggest hole. Aerobic base is there; no back reps on record. Every meet should include a Back event.
2. **Start racing Butterfly** — not to win, just to have data. Can't grow 200 IM without fly competence.
3. **Race 100 SC Free** — missing benchmark.
4. **Protect the breast progression** — don't over-tinker. Drops every meet she races 100 SC Breast.
5. **Get updated LC times** — Lou Pocock + Summer LC Inv.
6. **Consider distance specialization** — one of very few 10yos who can complete 1500 Free. Lean into it.

### What to watch for

- **Lou Pocock (when published):** does she race Back or Fly? If not, avoidance is structural. Does she race 100 LC Breast? Compare against the stale 2:01.30.
- **Summer LC Inv (May 22–24):** 200+ LC Free time? SC base suggests 3:10–3:20 range.
- **Any 100/200 IM LC attempt** — no LC IM swims; test piece for future profile.

<!-- ANALYSIS:END -->
